title: Tool Use: Function Calling
concept: Function calling lets the LLM output a structured JSON command (e.g. calling a weather API) when it needs external information, acting as a bridge to software.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use function calling in AI developers use function calling is a key concept in AI. Why do we use it?

options.text: To connect LLMs with external tools and APIs
To make the AI speak in different languages
To train the AI on new books

exercises.explain: Function calling allows LLMs to connect with external software, APIs, and databases by outputting structured data that code can execute.

cards.title: Bridging the Gap
cards.body: AI models are brilliant, but they don't know today's weather or your current account balance because they can't browse the live web on their own. What if they could generate structured 'buttons' to control external software and fetch that information? That is the power of Function Calling!

cards.title: What is Function Calling?
cards.body: Function calling is a technique where an LLM detects when it needs external data and outputs a structured request (usually in JSON format) containing function names and arguments. This acts as a clear bridge, telling external software exactly what action to take next.

cards.title: The Smart Restaurant Order
cards.body: Think of the LLM as a restaurant guest and function calling as the waiter writing down a structured order. Instead of the guest going into the kitchen to cook, they write down exactly what they want (like "burger, no onions") on a structured order slip. The kitchen (external API) reads this exact format, prepares the meal, and sends it back to the guest.

cards.title: From Question to JSON
cards.body: If you ask: "What is the weather in Paris?", the LLM realizes it doesn't have live data. Instead of guessing, it outputs a structured message: {"name": "get_weather", "arguments": {"location": "Paris"}}. Your app sees this, runs the actual weather API, and returns the sunny results to the AI!

cards.title: The AI Doesn't Run the Code!
cards.body: Many people think the LLM directly executes the code to query databases or send emails. In reality, the LLM only generates the formatted request (the blueprint). Your application code is the one that actually runs the function and hands the results back to the LLM.

cards.title: Text vs. Structured Action
cards.body: See how function calling translates conversational language into structured machine commands.

left.text: 'I think you should look up the weather in Tokyo right now.'

exercises.prompt: What is the primary purpose of an LLM generating a function call?

options.text: To write a creative story for the user.
To provide structured instructions that external software can easily run.
To make the AI think faster during conversations.
To delete older data from its training set.

exercises.explain: Function calling provides structure (like JSON) so that external APIs and databases can understand the request without parsing conversational human text.

exercises.prompt: Without function calling or tools, standard LLMs do not have access to live, real-time database information.
exercises.explain: The AI cannot browse the live web or access your local databases unless you give it tools and use function calling to bridge the gap.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank to complete the statement about the output format of function calling.
exercises.sentence: When an AI decides to call a tool, it outputs a structured command, usually in the format of a ____ object.
exercises.explain: Function calling outputs data in structured formats like JSON because software systems need rigid, predictable keys and values to run functions correctly.

exercises.prompt: Identify the output that represents a function call generated by an LLM.

options.text: Text: 'I can help you check the weather in Seattle! Let me look that up for you.'

exercises.explain: Option B shows a structured tool request containing a function name and arguments, which is a classic function call. Option A is just conversational chat, and Option C is plain computer code.

exercises.prompt: When an AI decides to use a function to fetch live flight data, who actually runs the code to call the flight database?

options.text: The LLM connects to and runs the external database query itself.
The LLM writes and executes the Python code on its own servers.
The LLM outputs structured instructions, and your application executes them.
The LLM guesses the database answers without needing any external tools.

exercises.explain: Function calling only writes down the structured instructions (the JSON request). It is the external software system or application code that actually runs the function and retrieves the real-world results to feed back to the AI.
